user_goal: Audit whether README.md accurately matches the current implementation across the repository, identifying confirmed matches, mismatches, and stale docs. Propose exact README corrections if needed.

mandates.title: CLI behavior and gating audit
mandates.objective: Verify README claims about interactive/inline workflows, dog-whistle mutation gating, and shell command behavior against orchestrator/engine code and tests.
method: Inspect orchestrator.py, ai_engine_main.py, inline_prompt_mode.py, inline_mode_renderer.py, bash_executor.py
Cross-check README usage and safeguards sections line-by-line
Cite concrete code evidence and identify mismatches
evidence_requirements: File:line references for each confirmed or mismatched claim
Distinguish exact match vs partial vs incorrect
non_goals: Do not propose stylistic rewrites unrelated to factual accuracy
stop_conditions: Core workflow claims are fully mapped to implementation

mandates.title: Tools and architecture audit
mandates.objective: Validate README Tool Suite and development architecture claims against ai_engine_tools.py, ai_engine.py, PROJECTSCOPE.md, and tests.
method: Inventory implemented tools and exported tool definitions
Check whether tool descriptions and behavior constraints are accurate
evidence_requirements: Concrete symbol/function names and file references
Call out stale or omitted features
stop_conditions: All Tool Suite and Development claims are reconciled

mandates.title: Config/env and install/release audit
mandates.objective: Verify README installation, upgrade, release, and environment variable claims against install.sh, config_loader.py, config_paths.py, ai_engine_config.py, and workflow files.
method: Check installer flags, default paths, upgrade semantics
Validate env var precedence and supported toggles
evidence_requirements: File path and behavior evidence for each claim

mandates.title: Repo-wide mismatch synthesis scout
mandates.objective: Perform broad repo scan for major implementation shifts (e.g., orchestra/multi-agent files) that conflict with README’s single-agent positioning.
method: Scan for orchestration modules and runtime entry points
Determine if user-facing behavior exposes multi-agent features
Classify conflict severity and remediation suggestions
evidence_requirements: List concrete files/classes/flags showing feature presence or absence
Explain whether mismatch is user-visible or internal-only
non_goals: Avoid duplicating deep checks already covered by others
